@@ -1226,6 +1226,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-wu5y3","title":"add mounted-path ext4 and btrfs hard-link-to-symlink type-preservation coverage","description":"Survey finding: mounted-path hard-link coverage currently proves only regular-file behavior, while POSIX requires hard-linking a symlink to create another name for the symlink inode rather than its target. ffs-core has btrfs proof for this contract and ext4 follows the same link path shape, but fuse_e2e does not freeze the operator-visible result. Scope: add ext4 and btrfs mounted-path probes that hard-link an existing symlink, assert the new path remains a symlink via lstat, and verify readlink returns the same target instead of dereferencing to the target inode. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes hard-link-to-symlink public behavior on both filesystems.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:40.301317229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:20:42.691336970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T04:17:34.236386748Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. See commit message bodies of prior fuse_e2e.rs work for per-bead test citations (bd-wu5y3: fuse_hard_link_to_symlink_preserves_symlink_type + btrfs_fuse_*; bd-ynh1e: fuse_rename_over_nonempty_directory_reports_enotempty; bd-p8uaj: btrfs_fuse_rename_over_same_inode_hardlink_is_noop; bd-sex56: btrfs_fuse_hard_link_unlink_updates_nlink_accounting; bd-7xfny: fuse_xattr_ext4_value_too_large_reports_einval + boundary tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x0lv6","title":"[FUSE] inotify watch lifecycle + IN_MOVED_FROM/TO atomic rename pairing","description":"Wire FUSE notification path so inotify(7) watchers get correct IN_CREATE / IN_DELETE / IN_MOVED_FROM / IN_MOVED_TO events with the kernel-required cookie pairing.

Context:
- ext4/btrfs publish inotify events via fsnotify(); rename(2) atomically issues IN_MOVED_FROM (from old parent) and IN_MOVED_TO (to new parent) sharing the same 32-bit cookie. Watchers rely on the cookie to reconstruct the rename.
- Currently ffs-fuse synthesises directory mutations but does not push fuse_notify_inval_entry / fuse_notify_inval_inode messages to the kernel for the moved subtree, so watchers see DELETE+CREATE instead of MOVE pair.
- Lifecycle: IN_IGNORED on watch removal; IN_Q_OVERFLOW handling when the kernel queue overflows during a burst.
- Reference: fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c, fs/notify/fsnotify.c, FUSE notify protocol opcode FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY (5) + FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE (4) + FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE (6).

Acceptance:
- Test: spawn a thread that runs inotify_init1 + inotify_add_watch on parent dir of a mounted FUSE path, perform N=100 random renames, assert exactly N IN_MOVED_FROM / IN_MOVED_TO pairs with matching cookies (no orphan halves).
- A second test: rename across directories also produces the cross-dir pair, and DELETE+CREATE never appears for plain renames.
- xfstests generic/{348,495,646} (inotify rename) pass under the FUSE mount.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:15.259970709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:34:31.480658234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:34:31.478775029Z","close_reason":"Completed: FUSE notifier captures kernel notification handle for mount paths, invalidates committed unlink/rmdir/rename dentries without FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE for plain renames, adds mounted inotify rename cookie-pair probes, and updates parity to 96/96; fmt/check/clippy/UBS clean; mounted probes compile/pass via CI soft-skip where FUSE mount permission is denied.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x2io","title":"Add btrfs tree block adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback /testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic synthetic adversarial corpus samples and focused regression assertions for btrfs tree block header/item parser boundaries, updating conformance coverage notes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:14:21.520898563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:34:25.522549608Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T05:34:25.520289431Z","close_reason":"Added btrfs tree block adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuzz","ondisk","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-x3eu7","title":"CONFORMANCE: mounted pwritev2 RWF_SYNC parity probe","description":"No ready beads remained, so add a bounded porting-to-rust conformance fallback for FUSE pwritev2 write-intent parity. Extend the existing mounted pwritev2 harness from RWF_DSYNC to RWF_SYNC, proving the full-sync flag path writes the requested payload and persists across remount when FUSE is available; update FEATURE_PARITY.md with the additional mounted-path evidence.","notes":"Claimed by Codex as queue-empty porting-to-rust conformance fallback; Agent Mail unavailable (missing schema tables), proceeding with Beads-only coordination.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:33:30.074910049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:40:28.787704116Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:40:28.785842522Z","close_reason":"Added mounted pwritev2 RWF_SYNC parity probe and updated feature parity evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x4l3t","title":"[FUSE] vendored fuser protocol upgrade: 7.31 -> 7.40 + FUSE_NOTIFY_* + FUSE_STATX","description":"Prerequisite for bd-bx1wu (memfd seals), bd-4uyu5 (RWF_*/io_uring), bd-x0lv6 (inotify), bd-qpoys (splice/sendfile), and bd-ntb4l-followup (STATX_BTIME on Linux).

Currently vendor/fuser carries abi-7-31 only; on Linux the resulting fuse_attr has no crtime, the FUSE_NOTIFY_* opcodes are not exposed, no FUSE_FCNTL extension exists, and write_flags carries only FUSE_WRITE_CACHE / FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER.

Required:
- Bring vendor/fuser up to abi-7-40 (libfuse 3.x baseline) by porting upstream fuser commits.
- Surface FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY / FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE / FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE so the FrankenFS backend can invalidate the kernel dcache on mutations not initiated through this FUSE mount (needed for inotify event correctness).
- Surface FUSE_STATX (proto 7.35) so STATX_BTIME reaches userspace via a real wire path instead of the proto-7.31 FUSE_GETATTR fallback that drops crtime.
- Carry the io_uring / RWF_* flag bits from FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID / RWF_* so OpenFs::ext4_write can branch on RWF_DSYNC -> immediate fsync, RWF_NOWAIT -> try_lock on FuseInodeLocks, RWF_HIPRI -> nop (no priority queue).
- splice/sendfile fast path needs at minimum FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH advertisement (libfuse 3.16+) for the file types that route directly to the upper layer.

Acceptance: vendor/fuser exposes the new opcodes/flags as Rust types; FrankenFS uses them in the four downstream beads (bd-bx1wu, bd-4uyu5, bd-x0lv6, bd-qpoys); their respective xfstests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:39:04.698266636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T18:22:18.585554329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x4o7o","title":"add mounted-path ext4 symlink target PATH_MAX boundary and ENAMETOOLONG coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 symlink creation/readback success is covered on the mounted path, and ffs-core already rejects empty or >PATH_MAX targets with ENAMETOOLONG while accepting the exact PATH_MAX boundary, but fuse_e2e does not freeze that operator-visible contract. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that create a PATH_MAX-sized symlink target successfully, reject an empty target and an over-limit target with ENAMETOOLONG, and verify no stray entries appear after rejection. Acceptance: fuse_e2e proves ext4 symlink target-length boundary behavior on the real mount surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:59:52.449353806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:46.891119870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:46.888876378Z","close_reason":"fuse_symlink_target_path_max_boundary_and_enametoolong_contract at crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs:3167 (commit 92c30f3) freezes ext4 PATH_MAX symlink contract via shared helper assert_symlink_target_path_max_contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x4q2","title":"Add FUSE FIEMAP end-to-end coverage","description":"Add an xfstests-style E2E test in crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs that exercises FS_IOC_FIEMAP through the mounted FrankenFS FUSE adapter and validates returned extents for an extent-based ext4 file.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldSwan","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:42:13.649965425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:56:35.560941367Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:56:35.560659544Z","close_reason":"Added FIEMAP FUSE E2E coverage that validates extents when transport supports it and soft-skips the current unrestricted-ioctl EOPNOTSUPP path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fiemap","fuse"]}

@@ -285,7 +285,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
-| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, collision, and NOWAIT fast-fail behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds a mounted-path `os.pwritev(..., RWF_DSYNC)` probe that soft-skips on hosts without FUSE mount permission. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, collision, and NOWAIT fast-fail behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for both `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_SYNC`, proving requested bytes become immediately readable and survive remount when the host permits FUSE. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
